seoTitle: Magenta vs Mahogany Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Magenta and Mahogany Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Magenta vs Mahogany Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Magenta and Mahogany Creek.
heading: Magenta vs Mahogany Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Magenta, Western Australia and Mahogany Creek, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mahogany Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Magenta (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Magenta or Mahogany Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Magenta has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Mahogany Creek scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Magenta scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Mahogany Creek scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Magenta or Mahogany Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mahogany Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
